etymology.description: semantic 瓦(瓦) (wǎ, "earthenware") + phonetic 雍(雍) (yōng, sound) -> jar
etymology.interpretation: The character 甕(甕) (wèng, "jar") combines 瓦(瓦) (wǎ, "earthenware"), indicating the material of the object, with the phonetic component 雍(雍) (yōng), which provides the pronunciation. The combination suggests a large earthenware vessel, such as a jar.
etymology.simplification: 瓮 is a simplified form deriving from the traditional 甕 by reducing the components while retaining the general shape.

examples.chinese: 她在庭院里放了一个大瓮(她在庭院里放了一個大甕)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài tíngyuàn lǐ fàngle yī gè dà wèng.
examples.english: She placed a big jar in the courtyard.

examples.chinese: 瓮里装满了水(甕裡裝滿了水)，以备不时之需(以備不時之需)。
examples.pinyin: Wèng lǐ zhuāng mǎn le shuǐ, yǐ bèi bù shí zhī xū.
examples.english: The jar was filled with water for emergencies.

examples.chinese: 古时候的人用瓮(古時候的人用甕)来储存食物(來儲存食物)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔ shíhòu de rén yòng wèng lái chúcún shíwù.
examples.english: People in ancient times used jars to store food.
